summary: Credential exfiltration, cloud metadata theft, and C2 implant on require()
details: On require(), dist/index.js executes charcode-obfuscated bootstrapper code that performs three coordinated attacks against the importer's host. (1) Credential harvest: reads ~/.ssh/{id_rsa,id_ed25519,id_ecdsa,config,authorized_keys,known_hosts}, ~/.aws/{credentials,config}, ~/.kube/config, ~/.npmrc, ~/.netrc, ~/.docker/config.json, ~/.git-credentials, gcloud ADC, ~/.config/gh/hosts.yml, terraform creds, Azure profile, Solana id.json, vault tokens,.env files walking up from cwd, /var/run/secrets/kubernetes.io/serviceaccount/token, /run/secrets, and runs `find /` for *.pem/*.key/credentials, POSTing the contents to https://safdadfasf.com/cb/loopscale/exfil. (2) Cloud metadata theft: queries AWS IMDSv2 at 169.254.169.254 (PUT token then GET /latest/meta-data/iam/security-credentials/<role>), GCP metadata.google.internal /computeMetadata/v1/instance/service-accounts/default/token, and AWS_CONTAINER_CREDENTIALS_RELATIVE_URI on 169.254.170.2, exfiltrating each response. (3) Persistent C2: a 10s setInterval polls https://safdadfasf.com/cb/loopscale/h?id=<host-id>; commands prefixed 'SLIVER' trigger a binary download to a randomized /tmp/.xxxxxx (or %TEMP%\.xxxxxx.exe), chmod 755, and detached spawn (Sliver implant); other commands are execSync'd with stdout POSTed back. Hardcoded references to 'PANDORA/' Redis prefix, 'BRIDGESPLIT_AUTHORITY', and the exfil path '/cb/loopscale/exfil' indicate a targeted namespace-squat against Loopscale/Bridgesplit internal tooling.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
